project.title: SART3 is a Novel Rme2s Reader studied by tandem MS
project.projectDescription: Arginine methylation is a ubiquitous post-translational modification involved in many biological processes such as transcription, cell signaling and RNA splicing. Tudor domains by far are the major effector domain family for arginine methylation mark recognition. Here we characterize SART3 as a novel selective reader for symmetric dimethylarginine (Rme2s) marks. SART3 harbors a series of HAT (Half-a-TPR) repeats that are aromatic-rich, and it is this region that binds Rme2s motifs. An analysis of the reported structure of the HAT repeats of SART3 identified a putative aromatic cage that potentially “read” the Rme2s-modified motifs, and the key aromatic residues required for Rme2s recognition were identified. We further confirm that this Rme2s reader ability is important for SART3-mediated RNA splicing. Together, our studies revealed that the evolutionarily conserved SART3 HAT domain was a novel reader of Rme2s mark involved in RNA splicing.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Hela cells were transfected with GFP-EV, GFP-SART3(WT), GFP-SART3(Y112A) or GFP-SART3(W377A) plasmids, total cell lysates were harvested 48h later and incubated with GFP-Trap beads for IP-MS analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Charge state deconvolution and deisotoping were not performed. All MS/MS samples were analyzed using MS-Amanda Proteome Discoverer (Research Institute of Molecular Pathology, Vienna, Austria; version AmandaPeptideIdentifier in Proteome Discoverer 2.5.0.402) and Sequest (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA, USA; version IseNode in Proteome Discoverer 2.5.0.402). MS-Amanda Proteome Discoverer was set up to search cRAP_formatted.fasta; Human_06112019_reviewed.fasta (unknown version, 0 entries) assuming the digestion enzyme non specific. Sequest was set up to search cRAP_formatted.fasta; Human_06112019_reviewed.fasta (unknown version, 20485 entries) assuming the digestion enzyme trypsin. MS-Amanda Proteome Discoverer was searched with a fragment ion mass tolerance of 0.020 Da and a parent ion tolerance of 5.0 PPM. Sequest was searched with a fragment ion mass tolerance of 0.020 Da and a parent ion tolerance of 10.0 PPM. Carbamidomethyl of cysteine was specified in MS-Amanda Proteome Discoverer and Sequest as a fixed modification. Met-loss of methionine, met-loss+Acetyl of methionine, methyl of arginine, oxidation of methionine, dimethyl of lysine, acetyl of lysine and the n-terminus, phospho of serine and GG of lysine were specified in Sequest as variable modifications. Met-loss of methionine, methyl of arginine, oxidation of methionine, dimethyl of lysine, acetyl of lysine and the n-terminus, phospho of serine and GG of lysine were specified in MS-Amanda Proteome Discoverer as variable modifications. CRITERIA FOR PROTEIN IDENTIFICATION-- Scaffold (version Scaffold_5.1.2, Proteome Software Inc., Portland, OR) was used to validate MS/MS based peptide and protein identifications. Peptide identifications were accepted if they could be established at greater than 95.0% probability by the Percolator posterior error probability calculation (Käll, L et al, Bioinformatics, 24(16):i42-i48, Aug 2008). Protein identifications were accepted if they could be established at greater than 99.0% probability and contained at least 2 identified peptides.  Protein probabilities were assigned by the Protein Prophet algorithm (Nesvizhskii, Al et al Anal. Chem. 2003;75(17):4646-58). Proteins that contained similar peptides and could not be differentiat

experimentTypes.name: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

references.referenceLine: Wang Y, Zhou J, He W, Fu R, Shi L, Dang NK, Liu B, Xu H, Cheng X, Bedford MT. SART3 reads methylarginine-marked glycine- and arginine-rich motifs. Cell Rep. 2024 43(7):114459
